sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.30 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is called {$$-subnormal} in$G$if either$H=G$, or there is a chain of subgroups$H_0 H_1 H_{n-1} H_{n} =G$such that$|H_i : H_{i-1}|$is a prime for all$i=1, , n$. Must a finite group be soluble if every Shmidt subgroup of it is$$-subnormal? { A.\,F.\,Vasil'ev, T.\,I.\,Vasil'eva, V.\,N.\,Tyutyanov} Yes, it must (V.\,N.\,Tyutyanov, Probl. Fiz. Mat. Tekhn., { 2015}, no.\,1 (22)...
